title: spathelia bahamensis
intro: Spathelia bahamensis, also known as Bahama spath, is a tropical plant species native to the Bahamas and other Caribbean islands, belonging to the Rutaceae family. Traditionally used in folk medicine, Spathelia bahamensis has been found to possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which contribute to its medicinal value. The plant's leaves and bark are known to offer various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and providing antioxidant properties. In herbal preparations, Spathelia bahamensis is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are believed to aid in relieving symptoms of colds and flu.
